failures.truthValue: deeper squadmate voice, grounded in-scene delivery

The trailer’s dialogue sets up Raul Menendez as the puppet-master behind fear, unrest, and psychological manipulation, while David and his squad are pulled through a surreal hallucinatory mission shaped by trauma, memory, and control. Corporate propaganda, military urgency, squad confusion, Menendez taunts, and Master of Puppets lyrics all reinforce the same theme: people are being manipulated like puppets. The dialogue arc ends with David choosing to confront that force directly, before the trailer shifts into a preorder promo.

Voice separation reminders:
- speaker_id continuity is acoustic, not semantic.
- Do not collapse scene dialogue, comms, sung lyrics, promo VO, and overlapping hallucination blends into one speaker unless the voice clearly matches.

- The mixed "You were never cut out to be a Mason" line is intentionally represented as an overlap-heavy speaker bucket rather than a falsely precise identity claim.

Voice separation reminders:
- speaker_id continuity is acoustic, not semantic. A speaker naming another person is not evidence they are that person.
- Before reusing any prior speaker_id, compare the audible match across vocal timbre, age impression, gender presentation, accent/dialect impression, delivery mode, and recording texture. If those cues do not clearly line up, create a new speaker_id.
- Do not merge narration/figurehead VO, antagonist taunts, female radio/comms, gruff soldier responses, and promo-announcer copy unless the voice itself clearly matches.
- Default official/public-address/newsreel/expository narration to a distinct speaker_id from villain threats unless the acoustic match is very strong.
- If delivery shifts from direct character/threat speech into official public-address, newsreel, briefing, or expository narration, prefer a new speaker_id unless the exact same voice clearly continues.
- If this chunk creates a new official/public-address/newsreel/expository speaker_id, keep the immediately adjacent follow-on official line on that same speaker_id unless strong acoustic evidence indicates another change.
- If adjacent words are one uninterrupted utterance from the same voice, keep them together instead of splitting them into artificial fragments.
- The handoff is reference-only memory. Never copy or continue prior lines unless they are audibly present in THIS chunk.
- If the current chunk only contains one audible promo/narration line, return only that line; do not invent follow-up tactical chatter from prior chunks.

fieldResults.truthValue: The trailer’s dialogue sets up Raul Menendez as the puppet-master behind fear, unrest, and psychological manipulation, while David and his squad are pulled through a surreal hallucinatory mission shaped by trauma, memory, and control. Corporate propaganda, military urgency, squad confusion, Menendez taunts, and Master of Puppets lyrics all reinforce the same theme: people are being manipulated like puppets. The dialogue arc ends with David choosing to confront that force directly, before the trailer shifts into a preorder promo.

Voice separation reminders:
- speaker_id continuity is acoustic, not semantic. A speaker naming another person is not evidence they are that person.
- Before reusing any prior speaker_id, compare the audible match across vocal timbre, age impression, gender presentation, accent/dialect impression, delivery mode, and recording texture. If those cues do not clearly line up, create a new speaker_id.
- Do not merge narration/figurehead VO, antagonist taunts, female radio/comms, gruff soldier responses, and promo-announcer copy unless the voice itself clearly matches.
- Default official/public-address/newsreel/expository narration to a distinct speaker_id from villain threats unless the acoustic match is very strong.
- If delivery shifts from direct character/threat speech into official public-address, newsreel, briefing, or expository narration, prefer a new speaker_id unless the exact same voice clearly continues.
- If this chunk creates a new official/public-address/newsreel/expository speaker_id, keep the immediately adjacent follow-on official line on that same speaker_id unless strong acoustic evidence indicates another change.
- If adjacent words are one uninterrupted utterance from the same voice, keep them together instead of splitting them into artificial fragments.
- The handoff is reference-only memory. Never copy or continue prior lines unless they are audibly present in THIS chunk.
- If the current chunk only contains one audible promo/narration line, return only that line; do not invent follow-up tactical chatter from prior chunks.
